raw_result.description: Customer service and helpdesk platform with ticketing, help center, and support APIs.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Zendesk API docs indicate OAuth access tokens and API tokens; API-token authentication is performed via Basic authentication. It is unclear from gathered evidence whether password-based basic auth remains broadly supported vs legacy/deprecated for all products, so this is marked for review.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing evidence shows API access as a plan feature on paid Zendesk plans such as Support Team, Suite Team, Suite Professional, and Suite Enterprise; no verified free self-serve API tier was gathered.

api_surface.breadth: Broad public API surface across Zendesk support/ticketing resources. Official docs gathered confirm a JSON-based REST API for the main Zendesk API, and browser evidence also found an official GraphQL query API for a Zendesk Sell/Sales CRM area, so surface appears mixed across product lines rather than GraphQL-first.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Browser evidence found third-party Zendesk MCP server repositories on GitHub, but no official Zendesk MCP server or official MCP registry entry was verified. Treat as community MCP only.

raw_result.blocker: API access appears tied to paid plans, and some details (official MCP status and exact auth scope across products) remain ambiguous from gathered evidence.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limits documentation indicates plan-based limits for Support and Help Center APIs, with browser-extracted evidence showing Zendesk Suite limits ranging roughly from 200 to 2500 requests per minute depending on plan, plus endpoint-specific limits and rate-limit headers documented on the rate-limits page.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs clearly expose authentication, request format, and rate-limit guidance. Pricing comparison shows API access as a visible developer-tools feature on multiple paid plans, and additional API volume appears available as an add-on for some larger plans. No official Zendesk MCP evidence was found; community GitHub MCP repos do exist.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Uncertainty remains on whether to classify auth as only oauth2+api_key versus also basic because API-token auth uses Basic headers; GraphQL support appears limited to a specific Zendesk product area rather than the main Support API; and no official MCP server was verified, only third-party repositories.

result.description: Customer service and helpdesk platform with ticketing, help center, and support APIs.
result.auth_notes: Official Zendesk API docs indicate OAuth access tokens and API tokens; API-token authentication is performed via Basic authentication. It is unclear from gathered evidence whether password-based basic auth remains broadly supported vs legacy/deprecated for all products, so this is marked for review.
result.gating_reason: Official pricing evidence shows API access as a plan feature on paid Zendesk plans such as Support Team, Suite Team, Suite Professional, and Suite Enterprise; no verified free self-serve API tier was gathered.

api_surface.breadth: Broad public API surface across Zendesk support/ticketing resources. Official docs gathered confirm a JSON-based REST API for the main Zendesk API, and browser evidence also found an official GraphQL query API for a Zendesk Sell/Sales CRM area, so surface appears mixed across product lines rather than GraphQL-first.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Browser evidence found third-party Zendesk MCP server repositories on GitHub, but no official Zendesk MCP server or official MCP registry entry was verified. Treat as community MCP only.

result.blocker: API access appears tied to paid plans, and some details (official MCP status and exact auth scope across products) remain ambiguous from gathered evidence.
result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limits documentation indicates plan-based limits for Support and Help Center APIs, with browser-extracted evidence showing Zendesk Suite limits ranging roughly from 200 to 2500 requests per minute depending on plan, plus endpoint-specific limits and rate-limit headers documented on the rate-limits page.
result.notable_signals: Official docs clearly expose authentication, request format, and rate-limit guidance. Pricing comparison shows API access as a visible developer-tools feature on multiple paid plans, and additional API volume appears available as an add-on for some larger plans. No official Zendesk MCP evidence was found; community GitHub MCP repos do exist.
result.human_review_reason: Uncertainty remains on whether to classify auth as only oauth2+api_key versus also basic because API-token auth uses Basic headers; GraphQL support appears limited to a specific Zendesk product area rather than the main Support API; and no official MCP server was verified, only third-party repositories.
